one_time_description: Acquisition of intangible assets (fishing licenses) totaling $9,446,180 during the year; auditor notes intangible assets of $14,395,680 are a departure from PSAS which would require expensing
economic_notes: First Nation operates gas bar, tobacco shop, fisheries (treaty and commercial), gaming, Enterprise/Heritage/Community Complex, gift shop, Cleanwave Restaurant. Investments in limited partnerships: MRDP (wind energy), ELP (surf clam), ANSMC Cannabis Holdings Partnership (41% interest in cannabis operations). Fishing licenses purchased as intangible assets.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED due to intangible assets (fishing licenses) of $14,395,680 recorded on balance sheet contrary to PSAS which requires expensing; accumulated surplus overstated by same amount. Large acquisition of fishing licenses ($9,446,180) funded by new BMO credit facilities ($8,949,991 long-term debt issued). Long-term debt nearly tripled from $5.58M to $13.97M. Three limited partnerships (wind energy, surf clam, cannabis) recorded at cost and not consolidated as not controlled. Four entities consolidated: Operating/Capital Funds, Trust Funds, Wagmatcook Enterprise Heritage and Community Complex, and Wagmatcook Education Authority Limited. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. Current liabilities calculated as operating loans $201,220 plus accounts payable $1,756,033. Expenses_enterprises estimated as sum of Fisheries treaty $214,080 + Fisheries commercial $3,003,849 + Gas Bar $3,738,916 + Gaming $518,892 + Enterprise Heritage Complex $1,362,714 + Tobacco $1,997,824 = $10,836,275 but reported separately in statements.
